purpose: Validate an opt-in native-MORI source-position weighted-scatter path that consumes flat route positions directly, while keeping the unset default behavior materialized and conservative.
source_change_summary: Add weighted_scatter_add_from_flat_positions(out, flat_positions, src, top_scores_flat, top_k, flat_position_offset).

The default backend is materialize, which reconstructs token_indices = (flat_positions - offset) // top_k and scores = top_scores_flat[flat_positions - offset], then calls the existing weighted_scatter_add path.
The opt-in backend triton_flat avoids materializing token_indices and score rows and computes token row and score lookup inside the Triton weighted-scatter kernels.
Native MORI source-position combine now calls the flat-position helper when source flat positions are available and records native_source_scatter_backend in timing metadata.
The direct TorchTitan launcher now passes CANARY_STANDARD_EP_SOURCE_SCATTER_BACKEND through to TORCHTITAN_STANDARD_EP_SOURCE_SCATTER_BACKEND and prints the selected backend in the run header.

remote_gpu_smoke.read: The unset backend exactly preserves materialized behavior. The opt-in Triton-flat path has the expected BF16 forward atomic-order difference when duplicate token rows are present, while gradients match this smoke.

timing_probe.scope: single-GPU production-shaped weighted scatter slice only; not an end-to-end training gate

materialization_followup.read: The shortest 8xMI350 native-MORI e2e gate with CANARY_STANDARD_EP_SOURCE_SCATTER_BACKEND=triton_flat failed upstream in native compact dispatch staging-stride/capacity handling before source-position scatter could be measured.

decision: Keep triton_flat opt-in only. The slice win is real but small, and this is not a promoted 8xMI350 throughput candidate. The larger native-MORI target remains final receive-layout collapse and compact helper VJP/return, because this patch only removes a source-position adapter edge.
